page_subtitle: Understand your solar need, savings, and financing in a simple way
hero_intro: This guide helps you understand rooftop solar, compare on-grid vs hybrid options, estimate your possible savings, and take the next step confidently.

education_blocks.title: What is solar rooftop
education_blocks.text: A rooftop solar system uses your roof to generate electricity from sunlight. Power is used in your home or business, and extra units can be exported where net metering is available.

education_blocks.title: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
education_blocks.text: This government scheme supports residential rooftop solar through subsidy and process support. Final subsidy amount depends on system size and policy at the time of approval.

education_blocks.title: Who is eligible
education_blocks.text: Generally, homeowners with suitable roofs, a valid electricity connection, and required documents can apply. Final eligibility depends on DISCOM and policy rules.

education_blocks.title: On-grid
education_blocks.text: On-grid systems are connected with the utility grid. They are usually best for reducing bills where daytime usage and net metering are available.

education_blocks.title: Hybrid
education_blocks.text: Hybrid systems combine solar with battery backup. They are useful when you need power during outages, though initial cost is usually higher.

education_blocks.title: Which one is suitable for whom
education_blocks.text: On-grid is often preferred for lowest upfront cost and strong savings. Hybrid is better when backup during power cuts is important.

education_blocks.title: Benefits
education_blocks.text: Lower electricity expenses, protection from tariff increases, cleaner energy, and better long-term control over your energy costs.

education_blocks.title: Important expectations
education_blocks.text: Actual generation and savings vary with roof direction, shadow-free area, weather, usage timing, and billing rules. Site survey and bill analysis are essential.

faq.q: Will solar make my bill zero?
faq.a: Not always. Most customers still see fixed charges or minimum billing components. Solar mainly reduces energy charges.

faq.q: What if I don't know my monthly units?
faq.a: You can still estimate using monthly bill and tariff. Entering units gives a more accurate recommendation.

faq.q: Which is better: loan or self-funded?
faq.a: Self-funded often gives better lifetime savings, while a loan reduces upfront burden and helps adopt earlier.

faq.q: Do I need a lot of roof area?
faq.a: Roughly 80-100 sq ft per kW is a common planning range, but actual design depends on module and layout.
